scenario: Forge Manufacturing operates Camunda 8.8 with 18 job workers handling 8000 jobs per hour. The team has set camunda.client.worker.defaults.fetch-variables to a list of 4 audit fields but also wants every worker to receive the entire process payload, so it is consulting the documented relationship to force-fetch-all-variables.
question: What does camunda.client.worker.defaults.force-fetch-all-variables do relative to fetch-variables in the 8.8 reference?

options.text: The reference documents force-fetch-all-variables as a boolean that overrides fetch-variables to pull every variable on job activation across all 8.8 workers.
The reference documents force-fetch-all-variables as a boolean that augments fetch-variables with the audit subset declared on each handler annotation.
The reference documents force-fetch-all-variables as a duration that throttles fetch-variables to a polling interval across all 8.8 workers globally.
The reference documents force-fetch-all-variables as a boolean that toggles auto-complete behavior whenever fetch-variables is non-empty on a worker.

a.text: Correct. The 8.8 reference lists force-fetch-all-variables as a boolean and notes it overrides fetch-variables so every variable is pulled on job activation.

b.text: Incorrect. The flag does not selectively augment the list; the reference says it overrides fetch-variables and fetches all variables instead.

c.text: Incorrect. The property is a boolean, not a duration; poll-interval is a separate property under the same defaults section.

d.text: Incorrect. The flag is unrelated to auto-complete; auto-complete is a separate boolean under camunda.client.worker.defaults in 8.8.

explanation: The camunda.client.worker.defaults.force-fetch-all-variables property is a boolean that, when true, overrides fetch-variables so the worker fetches every process variable on job activation.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
